executive_summary.recommended_approach: Integrate Autonomize AI's PA Copilot with the health plan's systems via an Azure-native event-driven architecture. AI-driven clinical review automates initial intake and determination with configurable confidence thresholds and mandatory human oversight for complex cases, reducing review time by 45% and achieving 50% auto-determination (based on Altais benchmarks).
key_benefits: 45% PA review time reduction demonstrated by Altais deployment of same platform (Feb 2026)
50% auto-determination rate with configurable confidence threshold — immediate operational relief
54% manual error reduction through structured AI-assisted clinical review
CMS-0057-F compliance readiness — FHIR R4 API foundation meets Jan 2027 deadline
Azure-native deployment leverages Autonomize Pegasus Program membership and Azure Marketplace presence
Proven platform — Autonomize AI live with 3 of top 5 US health plans
key_risks: Fax OCR accuracy on handwritten clinical notes may require manual fallback for complex documents
Legacy clinical database integration complexity — requires per-system connector development during discovery
Auto-denial excluded from initial deployment — regulatory and clinical safety review required before activation
Autonomize platform specifics require discovery — no public API documentation available
executive_summary.total_investment_range: Investment dependent on discovery findings and team composition — see progressive delivery phases for scope breakdown

llm.primary_model: Claude Opus 4.6 (complex clinical review, multi-step reasoning)
llm.hosting: Azure AI Foundry (Microsoft Foundry) — East US 2
llm.rationale: Claude available in Azure AI Foundry with healthcare connectors (CMS Coverage DB, ICD-10, NPI Registry). Azure is the only cloud with both Claude and GPT frontier models. Autonomize platform is Azure-native (Pegasus Program, Azure Marketplace). Healthcare-specific skills available at github.com/anthropics/healthcare.

orchestration.framework: Autonomize AI Genesis Platform + Azure AI Foundry Agent Service
orchestration.rationale: Single orchestrator agent with specialized skills/tools (ReAct pattern). Autonomize PA Copilot as the core AI engine with configurable workflow. Azure Foundry Agent Service provides MCP support, observability, and enterprise security. Agent skills include: clinical data extraction, coverage criteria matching, eligibility verification, determination reasoning.

frontend.rationale: Autonomize AI Studio provides low-code workflow customization and clinical review UI. Demo uses simple web interface (Streamlit or HTML/JS).

primary_database.purpose: PA request records, determination history, audit logs, workflow state

vector_store.technology: Azure AI Search (vector search) or pgvector extension
vector_store.purpose: Clinical guideline embeddings, coverage criteria search, semantic matching for clinical evidence

document_store.purpose: Fax images, clinical documents, PDF attachments, OCR results

cache.purpose: Session state, eligibility lookup cache, rate limiting

message_queue.technology: Azure Service Bus (Premium tier — HIPAA BAA covered)
message_queue.purpose: Asynchronous PA request processing, event-driven workflow orchestration, component decoupling. Kafka mentioned as future scale option.

infrastructure.compute: Azure Container Apps (production) / Azure App Service (demo)
infrastructure.region: East US 2 (primary) — co-located with Azure AI Foundry Claude models
infrastructure.logging: Azure Monitor Logs (centralized, structured, HIPAA-compliant retention)

component_design.name: PA Request Ingestion Gateway
component_design.purpose: Securely receives PA requests from all channels (fax, web portal, X12 278 EDI) and normalizes them into a canonical PA request format for downstream processing.
outputs: Normalized PA request (JSON) on Azure Service Bus
component_design.scalability: Horizontal — Azure Functions auto-scale by request volume

component_design.name: Document Processing Pipeline
component_design.technology: Azure AI Document Intelligence + Autonomize AI
component_design.purpose: Extracts structured data from unstructured PA submissions — OCR for faxes, parsing for PDFs, clinical note extraction. Converts to structured format for AI engine.
outputs: Structured clinical data (patient demographics, diagnosis codes, procedure requests, clinical evidence)

component_design.name: Member Eligibility Service
component_design.technology: REST API integration with Payer Core System
component_design.purpose: Validates member eligibility, retrieves benefit details, and confirms PA requirements for the requested service. First integration point — blocks processing if member is ineligible.
component_design.scalability: Depends on Payer Core System API capacity — add caching layer

component_design.name: Clinical Data Aggregation Service
component_design.technology: Azure Health Data Services (FHIR R4) + Legacy DB Connectors
component_design.purpose: Retrieves and standardizes clinical data needed for AI review — pulls from FHIR R4 endpoints and legacy databases, presents unified clinical context to the AI engine.
outputs: Aggregated clinical context (FHIR R4 format): diagnoses, labs, procedures, clinical notes

component_design.name: Autonomize PA Copilot (AI Engine)
component_design.technology: Autonomize AI Genesis Platform + Claude (via Azure AI Foundry)
component_design.purpose: Core AI engine — reviews clinical evidence against coverage criteria, generates determination recommendation with confidence score and reasoning. Uses ReAct pattern with specialized skills: clinical data extraction, coverage matching, medical necessity assessment.
component_design.scalability: Horizontal — scales with LLM API throughput and concurrent agent instances

component_design.name: Determination Router
component_design.purpose: Routes AI determination based on confidence threshold: high-confidence approvals auto-processed, low-confidence or denial cases routed to human clinical reviewer. Configurable per LOB.

component_design.name: Clinical Review Dashboard
component_design.purpose: Human clinical reviewer interface — presents AI recommendation, supporting evidence, and clinical context. Reviewer confirms, modifies, or overrides AI determination. Feedback captured for model improvement.
component_design.scalability: Scales with reviewer count — web-based, concurrent access

component_design.name: Determination Response Service
component_design.purpose: Writes final PA determination back to Payer Core System and sends response to requesting provider via appropriate channel (portal notification, X12 278 response, fax).

component_design.name: LLMOps Pipeline
component_design.technology: Azure Monitor + Custom eval framework + Autonomize AI
component_design.purpose: Monitors AI model performance in production — tracks accuracy, hallucination rate, clinical safety metrics. Runs automated evals on determination samples. Captures human reviewer feedback for continuous improvement.

component_design.name: Audit and Compliance Service
component_design.technology: Azure Monitor Logs + Azure Blob Storage (immutable)
component_design.purpose: Maintains tamper-proof audit trail for all PA decisions — AI reasoning, human review actions, determination outcomes. Supports HIPAA audit requirements and regulatory reporting.
inputs: All system events: PA submissions, AI determinations, human reviews, final decisions

data_flows.name: PA Request Submission
data_flows.data_type: PA request (fax image, portal form, EDI transaction)

data_flows.name: Clinical Data Retrieval
data_flows.protocol: FHIR R4 REST API + Legacy DB connectors

data_flows.name: AI Clinical Review
data_flows.data_type: Structured PA request + clinical evidence + coverage criteria

data_flows.name: Human Review Queue
data_flows.data_type: PA case with AI recommendation for human review

data_flows.name: Audit Trail
data_flows.data_type: System events, AI decisions, human actions

services.compute: Azure Container Apps (production), Azure App Service (demo)
services.ai_platform: Azure AI Foundry (Claude models, Agent Service, MCP support)
services.storage: Azure Blob Storage (immutable for audit, standard for documents)
services.identity: Microsoft Entra ID (RBAC, SMART on FHIR)
services.networking: Azure Virtual Network, Private Endpoints, Azure API Management

cloud_infrastructure.deployment_pattern: Container-based with event-driven processing. Azure Container Apps for stateless services, Azure Functions for event handlers, Azure Service Bus for async messaging.

backup_dr.strategy: Azure-native geo-redundant storage, PostgreSQL read replicas, Service Bus geo-disaster recovery

cloud_infrastructure.cost_optimization: Start with smallest viable SKUs, auto-scale based on PA volume. Azure AI Foundry pay-per-token (no reserved capacity needed). Service Bus Premium only where HIPAA requires it.

ai_ml_components.ai_engine: Autonomize PA Copilot on Genesis Platform — compound AI with multiple specialized skills
ai_ml_components.llm_provider: Claude (Anthropic) via Azure AI Foundry — Opus for complex review, Sonnet for routine
ai_ml_components.orchestration_pattern: Single orchestrator agent with ReAct loop: receive PA → check eligibility → retrieve clinical data → match coverage criteria → generate determination → route based on confidence
agent_skills: Clinical data extraction (NLP on unstructured notes)
ICD-10 / CPT code validation (Claude healthcare connector)
CMS Coverage DB lookup (Claude healthcare connector)
guardrails: Output validation — determination must include confidence score, reasoning, and evidence citations
Prompt injection defense — input sanitization, system prompt isolation
Hallucination detection — cross-reference AI claims against retrieved clinical data
Clinical safety — never auto-deny without human review (Phase 1)
PHI minimization — only pass necessary clinical data to LLM, tokenize identifiers
ai_ml_components.human_in_the_loop: Confidence threshold routing: high confidence (>configurable threshold) → auto-approve, below threshold → human clinical review queue. All denials require human review in Phase 1.

logging.phi_handling: PHI logged only to HIPAA-compliant destinations, tokenized in general logs

key_metrics: Human review queue depth and wait time
LLM token usage and cost per determination

tracing.scope: End-to-end request tracing from PA submission through determination response
tracing.correlation: Trace ID propagated across all components via Azure Service Bus message properties

notes.placeholder: Scores will be populated by parallel-wa-reviewer sub-agents

well_architected_scores.score_source: pending — parallel-wa-reviewer sub-agents to be invoked
well_architected_scores.calibration_note: Scores will reflect independent parallel agent review. Placeholder zeros will be replaced.

alternatives_considered.name: Apache Kafka for message queuing
alternatives_considered.summary: Kafka provides higher throughput event streaming with log compaction and replay capabilities
alternatives_considered.why_rejected: Azure Service Bus is simpler to operate, HIPAA BAA covered on Premium tier, sufficient for PA volumes. Kafka recommended as future scale option if throughput exceeds Service Bus capacity. Aligns with Phase 0 decision TD-3.

alternatives_considered.name: Multi-agent architecture (separate agents per skill)
alternatives_considered.summary: Dedicated agents for OCR, eligibility, clinical review, determination — each with own LLM context
alternatives_considered.why_rejected: Single orchestrator agent with skills is simpler, reduces inter-agent coordination overhead, aligns with Autonomize PA Copilot compound AI model. Multi-agent scales better at 80+ concurrent tasks (Mount Sinai research) but PA workflow is sequential, not parallel. Phase 0: single agent with skills preferred.

alternatives_considered.name: AWS-primary deployment
alternatives_considered.summary: Paul has 5 AWS certifications and deep AWS experience. AWS has SageMaker, Bedrock, HealthLake.
alternatives_considered.why_rejected: Autonomize AI is Azure-native (Pegasus Program, Azure Marketplace, Azure deployments confirmed). Interview is for Autonomize AI role. Azure AI Foundry provides Claude + GPT models. AWS experience translates to Azure concepts. Phase 0 decision TD-1.

alternatives_considered.name: LangChain/LangGraph for orchestration
alternatives_considered.summary: Popular open-source framework for LLM application development
alternatives_considered.why_rejected: Autonomize Genesis Platform provides healthcare-specific orchestration. Anthropic SDK (native) preferred for Claude integration with healthcare connectors. Adding LangChain creates unnecessary abstraction layer. Industry direction: native SDKs over frameworks for single-provider deployments.

alternatives_considered.name: Detailed FHIR R4 / Da Vinci Implementation Guide conformance
alternatives_considered.summary: Full SMART on FHIR integration with Da Vinci Prior Auth IG
alternatives_considered.why_rejected: Phase 0 decision TD-4: FHIR R4 label only, limited depth. Paul's FHIR experience is at label level. Deep FHIR conformance is a discovery-phase activity with clinical informaticists, not an architecture decision.

_metadata.author: sa-agent
_metadata.design_method: Generic-first design with Azure mapping per Phase 0 decisions
